commitments.Concise Description: Deployment of SANDF to support SAPS against gang violence and illegal mining.
Exact Quotes: I am deploying the South African National Defence Force (SANDF) to support the police, as we did to great effect with illegal mining.

I have directed the Minister of Police and the SANDF to develop a tactical plan where our security forces should be deployed within the next few days in the Western Cape and Gauteng to deal with gang violence and illegal mining.
commitments.Reasoning: Organised crime is an immediate threat to democracy and economic development; previous success with illegal mining indicates effectiveness.

commitments.Concise Description: Stepping up the fight against organised crime by consolidating intelligence and deploying intervention teams.
Exact Quotes: Our primary focus this year is stepping up the fight against organised crime and criminal syndicates using technology, intelligence and integrated law enforcement.

We will tackle organised crime by consolidating intelligence at national level, identifying priority syndicates and deploying hand-picked, multi-disciplinary intervention teams focused on dismantling criminal networks.
commitments.Reasoning: Organised crime is the most immediate threat to democracy, society, and economic development.

Exact Quotes: We are going to tackle gun crime by streamlining legislation and regulations on licencing, possessing and trading in firearms and ammunition.
commitments.Reasoning: To rid the country of gang violence and tackle gun crime.

Exact Quotes: We are putting more boots on the ground through the recruitment this year of 5 500 additional police officers...
commitments.Reasoning: To increase police presence and enforcement capabilities.

commitments.Concise Description: Establishing a National Illicit Economy Disruption Programme.
Exact Quotes: We are establishing a National Illicit Economy Disruption Programme that brings together key state agencies and other stakeholders, including the private sector.
Through effective use of data analytics and Artificial Intelligence, we will target high-risk sectors like tobacco, fuel, alcohol and other counterfeit products.
commitments.Reasoning: To address the threat posed by the infiltration of illegal and counterfeit goods to South African jobs and industry.

commitments.Concise Description: Re-vetting Senior Management of SAPS and Metro Police departments.
Exact Quotes: The State Security Agency will re-vet the Senior Management of the SAPS and Metro Police departments. The vetting process will include lifestyle audits.
commitments.Reasoning: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry exposed rampant corruption and abuse of power in SAPS and some Metro Police departments.

commitments.Concise Description: Establish a new criminal justice reform initiative.
Exact Quotes: we will use a similar approach to establish a hard-hitting new criminal justice reform initiative. This will crack down on organised crime, corruption and the illicit firearms trade using a dedicated team in The Presidency to ensure that reforms are implemented across the system.
commitments.Reasoning: To ensure reforms are implemented effectively across the criminal justice system.

commitments.Concise Description: Finalise approach to establishing a permanent, independent anti-corruption body.
Exact Quotes: We will finalise government's approach to the recommendation of the National Anti-Corruption Advisory Council on the establishment of a permanent, independent, overarching anti-corruption body.
commitments.Reasoning: To further intensify the fight against corruption.

commitments.Concise Description: Introduce the Whistle-Blower Protection Bill in Parliament.
Exact Quotes: The Whistle-Blower Protection Bill will be introduced in Parliament. Among other things, this will criminalise retaliation and provide psychosocial, legal and financial support to whistle-blowers.
commitments.Reasoning: To stop the victimisation and targeting of those who speak out against corruption.

commitments.Concise Description: Finalisation of new Public Procurement Act regulations.
Exact Quotes: Measures will include the use of technology and the finalisation of new Public Procurement Act regulations by mid-2026.
commitments.Reasoning: To end corruption in the procurement system, which is where the majority of incidents originate according to the Auditor-General.

commitments.Concise Description: Public funding for water and sanitation infrastructure.
Exact Quotes: We have committed more than R156 billion in public funding for water and sanitation infrastructure over the next three years.
commitments.Reasoning: To ensure water security in the long term by building new dams and upgrading existing infrastructure.

commitments.Concise Description: Establishment of a National Water Resource Infrastructure Agency.
Exact Quotes: we are in the final stages of establishing a National Water Resource Infrastructure Agency to effectively manage and mobilise funding for the country's water infrastructure.
commitments.Reasoning: To effectively manage and mobilise funding for the country's water infrastructure.

commitments.Concise Description: Introduction of the Water Services Amendment Bill.
Exact Quotes: The Water Services Amendment Bill will enable us to hold water service providers accountable for their performance and withdraw their license if they fail to deliver.
commitments.Reasoning: To address root causes of the water crisis and failures in municipality performance.

Concise Description: Deploy technical experts and resources to municipalities facing water challenges.
Exact Quotes: It will deploy technical experts and resources from national government to municipalities facing water challenges.
Reasoning: To ensure that action is taken swiftly and effectively to address the problem.

Concise Description: Lay criminal charges against Municipal Managers in their personal capacity for violating the National Water Act.
Exact Quotes: We will now move to lay charges against Municipal Managers in their personal capacity for violating the National Water Act of 1998.
Reasoning: To hold to account those who neglect their responsibility of supplying water to our people.

Concise Description: Incentive for metros to reform water, sanitation, and electricity services.
Exact Quotes: we have introduced a new R54 billion incentive for metros to reform their water, sanitation and electricity services.
Reasoning: To ensure that revenue from water usage is put straight back into fixing pipes, reservoirs and pumping stations.

Concise Description: Propose fundamental changes to the municipal system with a differentiated approach to powers and responsibilities.
Exact Quotes: We will propose fundamental changes that recognise the reality that some municipalities can take on more functions than others, and that we need a differentiated approach to municipal powers and responsibilities.
Reasoning: The current system is too complex and fragmented, expecting even small and weak municipalities to take on many responsibilities.

Concise Description: Ensure senior local government officials are qualified and appointed through an independent process.
Exact Quotes: We will ensure that senior officials in local government have the required qualifications and are appointed through an independent process free from political interference.
Reasoning: To address the issue where local government administrations are weak and governed by patronage rather than technical capacity and merit.

Concise Description: Public investment to build and maintain infrastructure.
Exact Quotes: Government has committed more than R1 trillion in public investment over three years to build and maintain infrastructure.
Reasoning: To change the decline in fixed investment and be transformative for jobs, productivity and growth.

Concise Description: Establish specialized courts for commercial matters.
Exact Quotes: we will establish specialised courts for commercial matters with dedicated judges and dedicated court rolls to ensure faster outcomes in matters that have a bearing on the economy and development.
Reasoning: To prevent undue delays in critical projects caused by disputes arising from implementation of tenders.

Concise Description: Establish a professional State Property Company.
Exact Quotes: This year, we will begin work to establish a professional State Property Company
Reasoning: Part of a comprehensive implementation plan to drive growth and inclusion.

Concise Description: Target for energy supply from renewable sources.
Exact Quotes: By 2030, more than 40% of our energy supply will come from cheap, clean and renewable energy sources.
Reasoning: To drive down the cost of electricity and ensure we are never again exposed to the risk of relying on a single supplier.

Concise Description: Establish a fully independent state-owned transmission entity.
Exact Quotes: We are restructuring Eskom and establishing a fully independent state-owned transmission entity.
Reasoning: To establish a level playing field for competition and have control of transmission assets and the electricity market.

Concise Description: Commence first round of independent transmission projects.
Exact Quotes: this year, we will commence the first round of independent transmission projects to enable private investment in expanding our national grid.
Reasoning: To modernize the energy system and enable private investment.

Concise Description: Eradicate load reduction.
Exact Quotes: We will work in each province to address transformer overloading, illegal connections and equipment failure with the objective of eradicating load reduction by next year.
Reasoning: To address local infrastructure failures and modernise the energy system.

Concise Description: Issue a Request for Proposals for high-speed rail corridors.
Exact Quotes: We are preparing to send out a Request For Proposals, which will introduce a new era of long-distance rail travel in South Africa.
Reasoning: To introduce high-speed rail in South Africa, covering routes such as Johannesburg to Musina and eThekwini to Johannesburg.

Concise Description: Deploy 10,000 new Extension Officers.
Exact Quotes: We will deploy 10 000 new Extension Officers to support farmers and improve agricultural productivity.
Reasoning: To support farmers and give rise to opportunities for young people to be employed in the agricultural sector.

Concise Description: Vaccinate the national herd of cattle against foot-and-mouth disease.
Exact Quotes: We have decided to vaccinate the national herd of 14 million cattle. This requires 28 million vaccines over the next 12 months.
Reasoning: To deal with the worst outbreak of foot-and-mouth disease which is damaging the economy and resulting in export bans.

Concise Description: Extend digital processing of tourist visas to all requiring countries.
Exact Quotes: In the coming year, we will extend the Electronic Travel Authorisation System to all countries that require a visa, enabling applications for tourists to be processed digitally within 24 hours.
Reasoning: To promote tourism as a vital driver of economic growth.

Concise Description: Introduce tax deduction for investment in new energy vehicles.
Exact Quotes: From March this year, we will introduce a 150% tax deduction for investment in new energy vehicles
Reasoning: To pivot the economy to green growth and support the local production of batteries.

Concise Description: Raise new investments over the next five years.
Exact Quotes: We have now set ourselves a target of raising R2 trillion in new investments over the next five years.
Reasoning: To build a more inclusive economy and back those who create opportunity.

Concise Description: Provide funding and guarantees to small and medium enterprises.
Amount: R2.5 billion funding; R1 billion guarantees
Exact Quotes: This year, we will provide more than R2.5 billion in funding to over 180 000 small and medium enterprises, and extend a further R1 billion in guarantees.
Reasoning: To back those who create opportunity and foster empowerment of women- and youth-led businesses.

Concise Description: Host the sixth South Africa Investment Conference.
Exact Quotes: We therefore look forward to hosting the sixth South Africa Investment Conference on 31 March 2026.
Reasoning: To follow through on investment targets.

Concise Description: Increase employment equity and procurement targets for persons with disabilities.
Exact Quotes: implementing decision to increase employment equity targets of persons with disabilities in the Public Service to 7% by 2030, and to mandate a 7% preferential procurement target across all government and public entities.
Reasoning: To ensure that no one is left behind.

Concise Description: Expand public employment programmes.
Exact Quotes: In the coming year, we will expand our public employment programmes, including the CWP, EPWP and the Presidential Employment Stimulus.
Reasoning: To provide income support, skills development and pathways into longer-term work, particularly for young people and women.

Concise Description: Make Grade R compulsory.
Exact Quotes: By making Grade R compulsory, we are getting all children off to a good start.
Reasoning: To establish a firm foundation for learning in the early years of a child's life.

Concise Description: Overhaul the skills development system to implement a dual training model integrating education and workplace experience.
Exact Quotes: We are undertaking a fundamental overhaul of the skills development system to implement a dual training model that integrates education with practical workplace experience.
Reasoning: The President explicitly identifies this overhaul as a priority to transform the current skills training approach.

Concise Description: Reform and reduce the number of Sector Education and Training Authorities (SETAs) to improve governance and alignment.
Exact Quotes: We will reform and reduce the number of Sector Education and Training Authorities to improve governance, strengthen industry participation, raise the quality of training and better align skills development with the needs of the economy.
Reasoning: Explicit commitment to structural reform within the skills sector.

Concise Description: Increase the skills development levy returned to employers to its original level of 40%.
Exact Quotes: To support effective workplace-based learning, we will increase the proportion of the skills development levy returned to employers, restoring it to its original level of 40%.
Reasoning: Specific fiscal commitment to incentivize employer participation in training.

Concise Description: Transform the National Skills Fund into an agile, outcomes-driven instrument for youth employment.
Exact Quotes: We will also transform the National Skills Fund into a more agile, outcomes-driven instrument that supports unemployed young people to access workplace experience and employment, building on successful initiatives such as Jobs Boost.
Reasoning: Commitment to institutional reform of the National Skills Fund.

Concise Description: Develop a proposal for building more universities and TVET colleges with specialized focus areas.
Who/Organization: Ministers of Finance and Higher Education
Exact Quotes: I have directed the Ministers of Finance and Higher Education to work on a proposal to build more universities and TVET colleges with specialised areas of focus.
Reasoning: Presidential directive to relevant ministers to plan for infrastructure expansion in higher education.

Concise Description: End child stunting by 2030 and tackle malnutrition among young children.
Exact Quotes: This year, we will embark on a mission to end child stunting by 2030 and tackle malnutrition among young children, in line with the National Strategy to Accelerate Action for Children.
Reasoning: Explicit long-term target to address a significant health crisis.

Concise Description: Implement targeted nutritional interventions for pregnant women and low birth-weight children.
Exact Quotes: Building on existing support such as the Child Support Grant, we will implement targeted interventions to ensure that pregnant women and low birth-weight children get the protein and nutrients that they need.
Reasoning: Commitment to specific health interventions for vulnerable groups.

Concise Description: Allocate specific funding for actions against child stunting in the upcoming budget statement.
Exact Quotes: The Medium-Term Budget Policy Statement will set out a clear allocation for the actions we need to take.
Reasoning: Fiscal commitment to support the mission against child stunting.

Concise Description: Implement measures to curb excessive alcohol use, including pricing, excise duties, and advertising restrictions.
Exact Quotes: As national government, we have proposed measures to curb excessive alcohol use, including minimum unit pricing or higher excise duties and greater restrictions on alcohol advertising, which we are consulting on with stakeholders.
Reasoning: Proposed policy changes to address the social impacts of alcohol abuse.

Concise Description: Continue the Social Relief of Distress (SRD) Grant and redesign it to support livelihoods and productive activity.
Exact Quotes: As a transformative instrument to improve the lives of the poorest and most vulnerable people, this grant will be continued. This year, we will redesign the grant to more effectively support livelihoods, skills development, work opportunities and productive activity.
Reasoning: Commitment to maintaining and improving a key social safety net.

Concise Description: Introduce a new housing model providing subsidies for ownership and rental in suitable areas.
Exact Quotes: We are introducing a new model for housing, where people are given subsidies for ownership and rental in areas that are suitable for them. We are shifting from building houses for people to supporting them to build, buy or rent their own housing.
Reasoning: Strategic shift in housing policy to empower citizens to secure their own housing.

Concise Description: Proceed with Phase 4 of the District Six restoration project.
Exact Quotes: With R500 million allocated to this work, we are proceeding with Phase 4 of construction.
Reasoning: Concrete funding and project phase commitment for historical redress.

Concise Description: Undertake substantial investment in health infrastructure, prioritising academic hospitals.
Exact Quotes: We will be undertaking substantial investment in health infrastructure, prioritising the construction and revitalisation of academic hospitals.
Reasoning: Broad commitment to upgrading the nation's healthcare facilities.

Concise Description: Finance the building and revitalisation of healthcare facilities, starting with George Mukhari Hospital.
Who/Organization: Government / Public and Private Financing Institutions
Exact Quotes: Starting with George Mukhari Hospital, we will be working with various public and private financing institutions to finance the building and revitalisation of healthcare facilities.
Reasoning: Specific project commitment for hospital infrastructure revitalisation.

Concise Description: Eliminate cervical cancer by ensuring girls aged 9-15 receive the HPV vaccine.
Exact Quotes: We have also committed to eliminate cervical cancer in our country by mobilising society to ensure that every young girl between the ages of 9 and 15 receives the HPV vaccine.
Reasoning: Clear public health commitment targeting a specific disease.

Concise Description: Scale up survivor-centred support for gender-based violence, including shelters and social workers in police stations.
Exact Quotes: We will scale up survivor-centred support, ensuring access to shelters, one-stop service centres, mobile and rural outreach and the placement of Social Workers in police stations.
Reasoning: Specific actions committed to support survivors of GBVF.

Concise Description: Prioritize funding for border security, covering infrastructure, technology, and personnel.
Exact Quotes: This year, we will take additional steps to secure our borders. Funding to strengthen border security will be prioritised, covering infrastructure, technology and people.
Reasoning: Policy and budgetary priority for national security.

Concise Description: Redevelop key border posts through public-private partnerships.
Exact Quotes: Key border posts will be re-developed through public-private partnerships...
Reasoning: Plan to use collaborative funding models for border infrastructure.

Concise Description: Extend Electronic Travel Authorisation to all international airports and the busiest land ports.
Exact Quotes: ...and will extend the Electronic Travel Authorisation to all international airports and the busiest land ports of entry.
Reasoning: Expansion of digital border management capabilities.

Concise Description: Protect key public service appointments from political interference via the Public Service Amendment Bill.
Exact Quotes: The Public Service Amendment Bill will protect key appointments from political interference and ensure that capable and qualified people are appointed to senior positions on the basis of their suitability for the job.
Reasoning: Legislative commitment to professionalising the public service.

Concise Description: Establish a central registry for disciplinary cases across all spheres of government.
Exact Quotes: We are establishing a central registry for disciplinary cases across all spheres of government.
Reasoning: Reform aimed at preventing employees with disciplinary issues from moving between state entities.

Concise Description: Finalise the National State Enterprises Bill and move towards a centralised model for managing SOEs.
Exact Quotes: We will continue to work in a phased manner towards a centralised model for managing our SOE portfolio... This includes finalising the National State Enterprises Bill in line with this approach.
Reasoning: Strategic reorganization of state-owned enterprise management.

Concise Description: Launch a Digital ID to enable secure access to digital services.
Exact Quotes: This year, the DHA will launch a Digital ID to enable safe and secure use of digital services for all South Africans.
Reasoning: Key project in the country's digital transformation agenda.

Concise Description: Digitise driver's licenses, Matric certificates, and Master's Office services.
Exact Quotes: We will digitise driver's licenses, Matric certificates and services at the Master's Office.
Reasoning: Modernisation of essential public services through digitisation.

Concise Description: Contribute to the implementation of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA).
Exact Quotes: We are contributing to the implementation of the African Continental Free Trade Area to expand trade between African countries, drive industrialisation and create jobs.
Reasoning: Commitment to regional economic integration and continental trade.

Concise Description: Launch the International Panel on Inequality with international partners.
Exact Quotes: ...we are working with our international partners towards launching the International Panel on Inequality.
Reasoning: Diplomatic commitment to addressing global inequality.

Concise Description: Withdraw troops from the UN Mission in the DRC to consolidate the Defence Force.
Who/Organization: South African National Defence Force (SANDF) / UN
Exact Quotes: We have requested the UN to allow us to withdraw our troops from the UN Mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo with a view to consolidating our Defence Force.
Reasoning: Strategic decision to reorganize and consolidate military resources.

Concise Description: Spread the National Dialogue process across all communities and sectors of society.
Exact Quotes: This year, the National Dialogue process will spread across South Africa, reaching every community, every school, every university and college and every part of our society.
Reasoning: Commitment to broad public consultation and consensus-building.

Concise Description: Convene a National Convention to consolidate public dialogues and guide future actions.
Exact Quotes: These public dialogues will culminate in a National Convention where all of these conversations will be brought together to outline the actions that we must all take to build a better South Africa.
Reasoning: Major consultative event to synthesize national goals.

Concise Description: Formulate a national compact and the next phase of the National Development Plan beyond 2030.
Exact Quotes: These conversations will guide the formulation of an overarching national compact and of the next phase of our National Development Plan beyond 2030.
Reasoning: Commitment to long-term strategic planning for the nation's development.
